scenes.reflectionPrompt: By the dawn. What new beginning are you seeking?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: An oath by the dawn, when light begins to overcome darkness.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Ten sacred nights. How do you value your time?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The ten nights are generally understood to be the first ten nights of Dhul-Hijjah.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Even and odd. Do you see the balance in all creation?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: By the even and the odd — all things created in pairs or singular.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: The night departs. What did it take with it?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: By the night when it passes on.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Is there in this an oath for one of sense?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: These oaths are profound reminders for those who reflect and reason.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Have you seen what your Lord did with 'Ad?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: Reflection on the powerful civilization of 'Ad that was destroyed for its defiance.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Iram of the pillars. What pillars are you building your life on?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: Iram, the city of lofty pillars, unmatched in its architectural splendor.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Unmatched in the land. Does uniqueness protect from judgment?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: A civilization the like of which had never been created in any land.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Thamud, who hewed rocks in the valley. What are you carving out for yourself?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The people of Thamud, famous for carving their homes into solid rock.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Pharaoh of the stakes. Power can be a trap. Are you free?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: Pharaoh, the owner of the stakes (symbolizing his military might or torture methods).

scenes.reflectionPrompt: They transgressed within the lands. Where do we cross the line?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: These civilizations exceeded all bounds of justice and morality.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Increased corruption. How do we plant seeds of restoration?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: And increased therein the corruption and mischief.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: A scourge of punishment. Are we prepared for the weight of our choices?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: So your Lord poured upon them a scourge of punishment.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Indeed, your Lord is in ever-watchful. How does that change your walk?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: Nothing escapes the Divine Watch; every deed is recorded and seen.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: When honored and blessed, we say 'My Lord honored me.' Is it a trial?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: Man often mistakes worldly blessings as a sign of Divine favor, forgetting they are tests.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: When restricted, we say 'My Lord humiliated me.' Is it a trial?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: Man also mistakes hardship as a sign of humiliation, forgetting it too is a test.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: No, but you do not honor the orphan. Who is waiting for your honor?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The true humiliation is not poverty, but the failure to care for the vulnerable.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: You do not encourage feeding the poor. What is holding back your generosity?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: A lack of social responsibility and compassion is a sign of spiritual failure.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: You consume inheritance greedily. What are we taking that isn't ours?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: Criticism of those who seize the inheritance of the weak and vulnerable.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: You love wealth with immense love. Does it love you back?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The excessive love for material wealth that blinds one to spiritual reality.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: No! When the earth has been pounded to powder. What will you stand on?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The final leveling of the earth, removing all its physical structures.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Your Lord comes with angels, rank upon rank. Can you imagine the Majesty?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The arrival of the Divine Presence and the ranks of angels for Judgment.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: When Hell is brought near. Will the reminder help then?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The realization and regret that come too late on the Day of Judgment.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Oh, I wish I had sent forth [some good] for my life! What are you sending forth now?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The profound regret of the soul for not preparing for its eternal life.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: None will punish as He punishes. Seek His mercy today.
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The absolute and unique nature of Divine punishment for the defiant.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: None will bind as He binds. Seek His freedom today.
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The absolute and unique nature of Divine binding and justice.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: O tranquil soul. Are you at peace with your Lord?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The beautiful address to the soul that found peace in faith and obedience.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: Return to your Lord, well-pleased and pleasing [to Him]. How sweet is that return?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The soul returns to its Creator in a state of mutual satisfaction and love.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: So enter among My [righteous] servants. Where do you belong?
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The invitation to join the ranks of the righteous servants of Allah.

scenes.reflectionPrompt: And enter My Paradise. Welcome home.
scenes.tafsirHighlight: The final entry into eternal bliss and Divine presence.
